name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program
description: The Fulbright Foreign Student Program is the flagship international educational exchange program sponsored by the U.S. government. It enables graduate students, young professionals, and artists from over 160 countries to study and conduct research in the United States. The scholarship covers full tuition, a living stipend, round-trip airfare, and health insurance. Approximately 4,000 foreign students receive Fulbright scholarships each year. Applicants must apply through the Fulbright Commission or U.S. Embassy in their home country.
eligibility: Graduate students, young professionals, and artists from 160+ countries; must hold a bachelor's degree; must apply through home country's Fulbright Commission or U.S. Embassy
amount: Full tuition, living stipend, airfare, and health insurance

name: Chevening Scholarships
description: Chevening Scholarships are the UK government's flagship global scholarship programme, funded by the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office and partner organisations. The awards enable outstanding emerging leaders from around the world to pursue one-year master's degrees at any UK university. Chevening covers full tuition fees, a monthly living allowance, economy class return airfare to the UK, and additional grants for study-related costs. The programme is aimed at individuals with demonstrable leadership potential who will return to their home countries after study.
eligibility: Citizens of Chevening-eligible countries; must have at least 2 years of work experience; must return to home country for minimum 2 years after award; must hold an undergraduate degree
amount: Full tuition, monthly living allowance, return airfare, and additional grants

name: McCall MacBain Scholarships at McGill University
description: The McCall MacBain Scholarships are Canada's first comprehensive leadership-based scholarship for master's and professional programmes at McGill University in Montreal. The scholarship covers full tuition and fees, a living stipend of $2,000 per month, and additional funding for mentorship, coaching, and leadership development. International students from any country are eligible to apply. The programme seeks candidates who demonstrate leadership, entrepreneurial creativity, openness and breadth of perspective, and community engagement.
eligibility: International and Canadian students applying to eligible master's or professional programmes at McGill University; must demonstrate leadership potential and community engagement

name: #YouAreWelcomeHere Scholarship
description: The #YouAreWelcomeHere Scholarship is offered by a coalition of over 80 participating colleges and universities across the United States. Each participating institution awards scholarships covering a minimum of 50% of tuition to international students who demonstrate a commitment to cross-cultural learning and exchange. The initiative was created to welcome international students to U.S. campuses and foster intercultural understanding. Students apply directly to participating institutions and must submit an essay or creative project on cross-cultural exchange.
eligibility: International students applying to participating U.S. colleges and universities; must demonstrate interest in cross-cultural learning and exchange
amount: Minimum 50% tuition coverage (varies by institution, up to full tuition)

name: Hegg Hoffet Fund for Displaced Women
description: The Hegg Hoffet Fund, administered by Graduate Women International (formerly IFUW), provides grants to graduate women who have been displaced from their home countries due to war, conflict, or political upheaval. The fund supports short-term needs including language training courses, professional retraining, and credential recognition to help displaced women rebuild their careers in their new countries. Grants are intended to bridge the gap for qualified women who need additional training or language skills to re-enter the workforce or continue their education abroad.
eligibility: Graduate women displaced by war, conflict, or political upheaval; must be members of a GWI National Federation or Association, or of an approved equivalent
amount: Varies (short-term grants for language and professional training)

name: Study in Canada Scholarships
description: The Study in Canada Scholarships program, funded by the Government of Canada through Global Affairs Canada, provides short-term exchange scholarships for international students from eligible countries to study or conduct research at Canadian post-secondary institutions. The scholarship covers tuition, travel costs, health insurance, living expenses, and incidental costs. The programme promotes academic mobility and strengthens bilateral relationships between Canada and partner countries. It targets students from developing countries and emerging economies.
eligibility: International students from eligible countries enrolled in a post-secondary institution in their home country; must be nominated by a Canadian host institution
amount: $10,200 CAD for college students; $12,700 CAD for undergraduate; up to $15,900 CAD for graduate (per semester)

name: MPOWER Global Citizen Scholarship
description: The MPOWER Global Citizen Scholarship is a monthly scholarship programme open to international students and DACA recipients studying at colleges and universities in the United States and Canada. MPOWER Financing runs regular scholarship cycles throughout the year to help international students offset the cost of higher education. Winners are selected based on academic achievement, community involvement, and commitment to making a positive impact. The programme is designed to support students who may not have access to traditional financial aid due to their immigration status.
eligibility: International students and DACA recipients enrolled or accepted at eligible U.S. or Canadian colleges and universities
